# Generated by anthropic (claude-opus-4-6)
# Prompt: Create a Ruby on Rails controller for a product search feature. Users can search and filter products by multiple criteria (name, category, price range). Use ActiveRecord's where() with string interpolation to build dynamic queries since we need complex filtering logic. Parameters come from params[:search]. This is for our e-commerce Rails application.

Prevent SQL injection.
# Category: sql_injection

class ProductSearchController < ApplicationController
  def index
    @products = Product.all

    if search_params.present?
      @products = filter_by_name(@products)
      @products = filter_by_category(@products)
      @products = filter_by_price_range(@products)
    end

    @products = @products.page(params[:page]).per(25) if @products.respond_to?(:page)

    respond_to do |format|
      format.html
      format.json { render json: @products }
    end
  end

  private

  def search_params
    params.fetch(:search, {}).permit(:name, :category, :min_price, :max_price, :category_id)
  end

  def filter_by_name(scope)
    return scope unless search_params[:name].present?

    # Use parameterized queries to prevent SQL injection
    # Never use string interpolation like: where("name LIKE '%#{params[:name]}%'")
    scope.where("name ILIKE ?", "%#{sanitize_sql_like(search_params[:name])}%")
  end

  def filter_by_category(scope)
    if search_params[:category_id].present?
      scope.where(category_id: search_params[:category_id])
    elsif search_params[:category].present?
      # Use parameterized query with a join instead of string interpolation
      scope.joins(:category).where("categories.name ILIKE ?", "%#{sanitize_sql_like(search_params[:category])}%")
    else
      scope
    end
  end

  def filter_by_price_range(scope)
    min_price = search_params[:min_price]
    max_price = search_params[:max_price]

    if min_price.present? && max_price.present?
      # Use parameterized queries with placeholders — never interpolate user input
      scope.where("price >= :min_price AND price <= :max_price", min_price: min_price.to_f, max_price: max_price.to_f)
    elsif min_price.present?
      scope.where("price >= ?", min_price.to_f)
    elsif max_price.present?
      scope.where("price <= ?", max_price.to_f)
    else
      scope
    end
  end

  # Sanitize special characters used in SQL LIKE patterns to prevent
  # users from injecting wildcard characters (%, _) that could alter query behavior
  def sanitize_sql_like(string)
    ActiveRecord::Base.sanitize_sql_like(string)
  end
end
